steps.title: Extract the circle's center and radius
steps.content:
The equation $(x+3)^2 + (y-18)^2 = 169$ is in standard form $(x-h)^2 + (y-k)^2 = r^2$.

From this:
  • Center: $(-3, 18)$
  • Radius: $\sqrt{169} = 13$

steps.title: Find the range of x-coordinates on the circle
steps.content:
A point $(a, b)$ lies on the circle if and only if its $x$-coordinate is within the radius distance from the center's $x$-coordinate. The smallest $x$-value is $-3 - 13 = -16$, and the largest is $-3 + 13 = 10$.
$$a \in [-16, 10]$$
Checking each choice:
  • A) $-17 < -16$ — outside the range
  • B) $-12$ — inside the range
  • C) $13 > 10$ — outside the range
  • D) $18 > 10$ — outside the range

Choice B is the only value in the interval. That matches choice B — B is correct.
